title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · Changed-Cluster Lab
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar namespace, failure domain, and policy constraint and must transfer authentication, authorization, rbac, service accounts, tokens, and impersonation into a new least-privilege identity and authorization policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-privilege identity and authorization policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Trace TLS identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request.
Separate external human users and groups, ServiceAccounts, node identities, workload identity federation, image pull identity, and bootstrap credentials.
Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace tls identity, authentication, user and group information, authorization, admission, validation, persistence, and audit for one request. For Commit to the mission model · Lab K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain, keep K8s Authn Authz Admission Chain connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A lab workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Security, RBAC, Pod Security, and Multi-Tenancy — Controls identities, authentication, authorization, RBAC, service accounts, bound tokens, Pod Security Standards and Admission, security contexts, Secrets, admission, isolation, and audit.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Token Audience Rotation Review
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-token-audience-rotation-review-2xueyq". Tailor its rbac-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Token Audience Rotation Review.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Token Audience Rotation Review into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab K8s Token Audience Rotation Review, keep K8s Token Audience Rotation Review connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-token-audience-rotation-review-2xueyq
  - competency: k8s-token-audience-rotation-review
    artifact: rbac-policy.yaml
    decision: token audience rotation review
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: remove DNS egress from an otherwise ready workload
    retained-proof: component versions skew drain timing and recovery rehearsal
    case-key: 2xueyq
    change: |
      serviceAccount: intake/status-api
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      token: projected-audience-intake-api
      role: get-configmap-status-only
      binding: namespace-intake
      review: auth-can-i-and-escalation-map
    verify: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api -n intake
    failure: Reject broad ClusterRoleBinding, long-lived static tokens, wildcard verbs, secret reads, bind or escalate paths, or workload creation as harmless.
    repair: Separate human and workload identity, scope verbs and resources, use bound audience tokens, test authorization, and review escalation periodically.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The K8s Token Audience Rotation Review structural check is only one gate. least-privilege identity and authorization policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
